title: mesona parviflora
intro: Mesona parviflora, also known as Vietnamese coriander or Mexican tea, is a species of flowering plant in the mint family. Traditionally, it has been used in herbal medicine for its medicinal properties, including its antimicrobial, anti-inflammatory, and antioxidant effects. The plant is believed to have various health benefits, such as reducing fever, relieving digestive issues, and supporting respiratory health. Mesona parviflora can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, or decoctions for consumption, as well as being used in traditional cooking and as a garnish.
